nodes.label: Ansible MCP Server - Advanced Ansible Model Context Protocol server.
nodes.norm_label: ansible mcp server - advanced ansible model context protocol server.

nodes.label: Existing Graph Fast Path
nodes.rationale: When graphify-out/graph.json already exists and the user asks a natural-language question, the skill should skip rebuild steps and query the graph directly to avoid unnecessary re-extraction.

nodes.label: Graph Health Check
nodes.rationale: The integrity gate is meant to surface dangling, missing, collapsed, and self-loop edges before labeling so silent corruption from AST/LLM ID drift or incremental updates stays visible.

nodes.label: Constrained Query Expansion
nodes.rationale: Queries are expanded only with tokens already present in graph vocabulary so traversal stays auditable and avoids hallucinated synonyms or cross-language guesses.

nodes.label: Incremental Update Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Incremental update saves time and tokens by re-extracting only changed files; code-only changes can skip semantic extraction, while doc, image, and video changes still require semantic work.

nodes.label: Watch Mode
nodes.rationale: The watcher can auto-rebuild code-only changes immediately, but docs and images only set a needs_update flag because semantic extraction still needs an LLM pass.

nodes.label: Copilot Instructions for Ansible MCP Server
nodes.norm_label: copilot instructions for ansible mcp server

nodes.label: Tool Definition Pattern
nodes.rationale: The repository guidance standardizes new MCP tools around typed signatures, kebab-case tool names, safe subprocess helpers, and dict responses with an ok field.

nodes.label: uv Package Manager
nodes.rationale: The repository positions uv as the default package and environment tool for faster, more consistent setup than pip-centric workflows.

nodes.label: SSE Transport
nodes.rationale: SSE mode complements local stdio by enabling remote Ansible servers and multiple simultaneous clients over HTTP server-sent events.

nodes.label: Docker Setup for Ansible MCP Server
nodes.norm_label: docker setup for ansible mcp server

nodes.label: Stdio Mode
nodes.rationale: Docker stdio mode is the default and is presented as best for local or integrated use with local IDE clients and simple setup.

nodes.label: SSE Mode
nodes.rationale: Docker SSE mode is optional and is presented as best for remote or network access with HTTP Server-Sent Events and an exposed port.

nodes.label: Production Considerations
nodes.rationale: The guide frames production deployment around security, health checks, logging, resource limits, networking, persistent storage, and security hardening.

nodes.label: Non-root User
nodes.rationale: Running the container as ansible:1000 is justified as a security measure to prevent privilege escalation attacks.
